input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.reasoningText: The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with a specific structure. This is a straightforward code generation request - no need to explore a codebase. Let me create the XAML and code-behind.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="YourApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:YourApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, top-level flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — single page, top-level flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace YourApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage));
        // or with a parameter:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage), typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating with a query parameter** (from either products tab):
```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

**Receiving the parameter** in `ProductDetailsPage.xaml.cs`:
```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    private string _productId;
    public string ProductId
    {
        get => _productId;
        set { _productId = value; LoadProduct(value); }
    }
    // ...
}
```

**Key points:**

- `FlyoutItem` creates a top-level menu entry. Nesting two `<Tab>` elements inside one gives you the bottom tab bar within that flyout section.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until the user navigates there.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is needed for pages **not** declared in the visual hierarchy (like your detail page). Pages already in the Shell tree get implicit routes.
- Use `..` in `GoToAsync` to navigate back: `await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("..");`
